title: cymbopogon citriodorus
intro: Cymbopogon citriodorus, also known as lemongrass, is a tropical and subtropical plant that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of lemongrass include its ability to reduce pain, lower cholesterol levels, and act as an antioxidant, offering various health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced stress, and enhanced immune function. Lemongrass can be consumed as a tea, added to food as a flavoring agent, or used in herbal preparations such as essential oils and capsules, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy.
